q: If the task is difficult, it is sweet to succeed. (Use 'The more/ less/ -er'.) 2026
a: The more difficult the task is, the sweeter it is to succeed.

q: If you spend more, you cannot save much money. (Join the sentences, using 'The more/less/-er..., the more/less/-er'.) 2025
a: The more you spend, the less money you can save.

q: If you spend time in nature, you will be free from stress. (Use 'The more/less/-er...') 2025
a: The more time you spend in nature, the freer you will be from stress.

q: If you are careful in your work and pay attention to details, you will not make many mistakes. (Use 'the more / less/ -er... , the more / less / -er...') 2025
a: The more careful you are in your work and pay attention to details, the fewer mistakes you will make.
